  • 3093830 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Release Restriction Note for Group Valuation

entry.title: Using Custom Logic in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to Realize Insufficient Stock Quantity at Outbound Deliver
entry.content:
Problem Background

A customer has requested a feature where, due to business management requirements, they want to automatically check whether the stock quantity of materials in outbound delivery line items is sufficient when creating the delivery. Specifically, when an outbound delivery is saved, the system needs to verify if the material quantity in the corresponding sales order line items exceeds the available stock quantity. If stock is insufficient, a Warning message should be displayed indicating the material shortage. Additionally, the customer wants to see the corresponding messages when creating outbound deliveries via API.

Solution

We implemented a custom logic solution using a combination of two BAdIs to meet the requirements.

Analysis Steps

1. Identifying the Appropriate BAdI Enhancement Points

First, we needed to find suitable BAdI enhancement points to implement the stock check functionality. After analysis, we discovered that the enhancement point LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK can execute final checks before the delivery is saved. Therefore, this BAdI can be used to return Warning messages when creating outbound deliveries in the frontend APP if stock quantity is insufficient.

2. Implementing Warning Messages for Frontend APP

We implemented basic stock check logic in BAdI LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK:

    CONSTANTS:
    lc_plant    TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
    lc_vkorg    TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
    lc_vtweg    TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
    lc_spart    TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

    DATA:
    lv_so_qty    TYPE menge_d,
    lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

    IF documentprocessingmode = 'CREATE'.
      LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item) .
      IF ls_item-plant = lc_plant.
      " Get Order Quantity of Sales Order Items
      SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
      FROM i_salesorderitem
      WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
        AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
      INTO @LV_so_qty.
      " Get Stock Quantity
      SELECT SINGLE MATLWRHSSTKQTYINMATLBASEUNIT
      FROM I_STOCKQUANTITYCURRENTVALUE_2( P_DisplayCurrency = 'CNY' )
      WHERE PRODUCT = @LS_item-MATERIAL
      INTO @LV_stock.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        CONTINUE. " No SO item found – skip this delivery item
      ENDIF.
      IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
        message-messagetype = 'W'. " saving of the delivery document will be warned
        message-messagetext = |Warning: The material has insufficient stock quantity|.
        EXIT.
      ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

3. Discovering API Scenario Limitations

However, this Warning message only displays in the frontend APP. Messages cannot be seen when creating deliveries via API. Therefore, we needed to implement an alternative solution.

4. Implementing Message Display for API Scenarios

To make the messages visible in API creation scenarios, we adopted the following approach:

Solution Details:

- Create a custom field that can be seen in the API Response
- Use BAdI LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE, which can modify standard and custom fields at both header and line item levels
- This BAdI is called before the delivery is saved to the database

Implementation Code:

IF documentprocessingmode = 'CREATE'.
  CONSTANTS:
    lc_plant  TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
    lc_vkorg  TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
    lc_vtweg  TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
    lc_spart  TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

  DATA:
    lv_so_qty TYPE menge_d,
    lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

  " -------------------------------------------------------
  " Step 1: Must first copy complete input to output (Cloud BAdI standard practice)
  " -------------------------------------------------------
  MOVE-CORRESPONDING delivery_document_in TO delivery_document_out.
  delivery_document_items_out = delivery_document_items_in.

  " -------------------------------------------------------
  " Step 2: Header organization data filter
  " Only applies to Sales Org 1310 / DC 20 / Division 00
  " -------------------------------------------------------
  *CHECK delivery_document_in-salesorganization = lc_vkorg.

  " -------------------------------------------------------
  " Step 3: Loop through delivery line items
  " -------------------------------------------------------
  LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item).

    " Skip line items without reference sales order
    *CHECK ls_item-referencesddocument IS NOT INITIAL.
    *CHECK ls_item-referencesddocumentitem IS NOT INITIAL.

    " Read order quantity from sales order line item
    SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
      FROM i_salesorderitem
      WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
        AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
      INTO @LV_so_qty.
    " Read material stock quantity
    SELECT SINGLE matlwrhsstkqtyinmatlbaseunit
      FROM i_stockquantitycurrentvalue_2( p_displaycurrency = 'CNY' )
      WHERE product = @LS_item-material
      INTO @LV_stock.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      CONTINUE. " No corresponding sales order line item found, skip
    ENDIF.

    " Compare stock quantity with sales order quantity
    IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
      " Update custom field
      delivery_document_out-yy1_message_dlh = 'Warning: Insufficient stock quantity for this material.'.
    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

5. CDS Views Used

During implementation, we used the following CDS Views to retrieve the required data:

- I_SalesOrderItem: Used to get the order quantity of sales order line items, queried by sales order number and line item number
- I_StockQuantityCurrentValue_2: Used to get the current stock quantity of materials, providing real-time stock information for materials at different storage locations, which is ideal for stock checking scenarios

6. Testing and Validation

After testing, the above code successfully meets the requirements:

- When creating outbound deliveries in the frontend APP, if stock is insufficient, a Warning message is displayed

 

- When creating outbound deliveries via API, the custom field value is updated, and the stock shortage message can be seen in the Response's custom field

 

Conclusion

By reading this article and several similar scenario blogs I've written previously, I hope you now understand how to implement stock quantity checks through custom logic when creating outbound deliveries, and how to return appropriate messages in different scenarios.

Follow this link to see more questions and blog posts related to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Sales: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales - SAP Community

If you have any suggestions or ideas about this article, please feel free to share them in the comments section. I look forward to exchanging ideas with you. Also, please follow my personal account, and I hope to see you in the next blog post!

entry.title: Ariba Sourcing Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP – End-to-End Business Process
entry.content:
Introduction

Organizations often spend considerable effort managing supplier quotations, evaluating bids, and manually creating purchasing documents. By integrating SAP Ariba Sourcing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP, businesses can automate the complete sourcing lifecycle—from creating a Request for Quotation (RFQ) to automatically generating a Purchase Order after supplier award.

In this blog, we demonstrate the end-to-end business process using the Automation of Sourcing with SAP Ariba Sourcing (Scope Item 4BL). The process shown here represents a simplified version of the standard 4BL business process. For complete configuration and setup instructions, please refer to the official Scope Item 4BL documentation.

The blog is built as a series of 2 parts and present the key highlights for:

  • Ariba Sourcing Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP – End-to-End Business Process
  • Technical configuration for integrating Ariba Sourcing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP

Business Process Overview

The sourcing process begins in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP, where a purchaser creates an RFQ. The RFQ is automatically transferred to SAP Ariba Sourcing, allowing strategic buyers to conduct the sourcing event, collect supplier responses, evaluate bids, and award the business to the selected supplier.

Once the supplier is awarded, SAP automatically synchronizes the result back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, where the corresponding purchasing document is created without manual intervention.

End-to-End Process Flow

Step 1: Create and Publish the RFQ

A purchaser creates a Request for Quotation (RFQ) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud using the External Sourcing Requirement option and publishes it.
This automatically creates a corresponding Sourcing Request in SAP Ariba Sourcing.

Step 2: Review and Approve the Sourcing Request

A Strategic Buyer logs into SAP Ariba Sourcing and:

  • Searches for the newly created sourcing request.
  • Completes the Prepare Sourcing Request task (if applicable).
  • Approves the sourcing request.
  • Opens the associated sourcing project.

This step ensures the sourcing request is ready for supplier collaboration.

Step 3: Publish the Sourcing Project

The Strategic Buyer reviews the sourcing project details and publishes the sourcing event, making it available for invited suppliers.
The sourcing event now becomes active for supplier participation.

Step 4: Supplier Participation

Suppliers log into SAP Ariba and perform the following activities:

  • Review the sourcing event.
  • Accept the participation terms.
  • Submit pricing and commercial responses.
  • Complete the bidding process.

Once submitted, the buyer can review all supplier quotations.

Step 5: Evaluate and Award the Supplier

The Strategic Buyer compares supplier responses and creates an award scenario.
After selecting the winning supplier, the sourcing event is awarded and the results are transferred back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Step 6: Automatic Purchase Order Creation

After the award is completed, SAP automatically creates the follow-on purchasing document in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Depending on the business configuration, this may include:

  • Supplier Quotation
  • Purchase Order
  • Purchase Contract (optional)

The purchaser can verify the generated document directly from the RFQ process flow.

 

Business Benefits

Integrating SAP Ariba Sourcing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud delivers several business advantages:

  • Eliminates manual transfer of RFQs between procurement systems.
  • Streamlines collaboration between buyers and suppliers through a unified sourcing process.
  • Accelerates supplier evaluation and award decisions.
  • Automatically creates follow-on purchasing documents, reducing manual effort and errors.
  • Improves procurement transparency with complete process traceability.
  • Standardizes sourcing activities using SAP Best Practice Scope Item 4BL.

Conclusion

SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP S/4HANA Cloud work together to provide a seamless, end-to-end sourcing experience. Procurement teams can manage sourcing events efficiently in SAP Ariba while relying on SAP S/4HANA Cloud to automatically generate purchasing documents after supplier award.

By automating the sourcing lifecycle—from RFQ creation to Purchase Order generation—organizations can reduce manual effort, improve procurement efficiency, and enable faster supplier onboarding and purchasing decisions.

For more information:

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2025-FPS1/SolP/4BL?region=DE

entry.title: 销售订单后续后勤活动控制
entry.content:
业务场景

1. 当创建销售订单触发审批流时，如果销售单未获批准或正处于审批流程中，则无需触发库存需求。在APP管理物料覆盖范围（及MRP有关APP）中，必须阻止或不显示该销售订单物料需求的转移。

2. 关于第三方销售订单场景，当销售订单创建且审批状态为“待审批”时，系统将生成该销售订单的采购需求。然而，若销售订单信息由多个部门共同审批，则内容可能存在修改。此时，销售订单中的交货日期、数量及其他采购相关信息需要进行调整。如果销售订单的审批状态为“待审批”时生成采购需求，此时采购部门将根据该需求向供应商发出采购订单。在审批完成后，若销售订单内容中对交货日期和数量进行了修改，则基于“待审批”需求发出的采购订单将与实际需求不符，严重影响企业的正常工作。需求控制第三方销售订单处于审批时，不生成采购申请。

3. 当销售订单被信用冻结后，要求物料需求不会被转至采购或生产环节。

无需手动操作即可将计划行类别更改为CN（无MRP）。当选择确认时的交货限制后，需求转移将被自动阻止。防止在受限订单中分配可用产品，并在确认限制期间阻止进一步的MRP（采购）处理。

系统实现

案例：Make-to-Order Processing

创建销售订单

点击行项目

字段Supply Block Reason设置Item not ready 01

此时订单总体供应链状态会是冻结

查看APP：库存物料清单MD04，发现销售订单11274物料需求没有进入

回到销售订单更改字段Supply Block Status到Not Blocked。

回到APP:库存物料清单MD04，销售订单11274物料需求进入

运行MRP：

生成生产计划订单：

注意：

一旦计划订单创建完成，供应冻结将不再影响MRP。客户可以继续将计划订单转换为生产订单。

建议:

客户可以通过检查未完整检查状态来决定是否设置字段：Supply Block Reason。

案例：第三方销售订单

当销售订单字段Supply Block Reason设置了Item not ready 01，保存后并不会自动生成采购申请

当销售订单更改字段Supply Block Status到Not Blocked，采购申请自动创建。

 

感谢观看

entry.title: Customize Your Emails for Situation Handling in the Extended Framework
entry.content:
Situation Handling

Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud alerts users about issues requiring their attention. You can use SAP-delivered templates or create your own use cases to detect critical business issues.

With the extended framework for Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608, you can customize the emails for object-based situations. This blog post explains the default notification settings and the customizing option.

Alert the right users about situations

Situation Handling uses multiple channels to inform the users about critical business situations that require their attention. This includes:

  • Situation descriptions embedded in business apps
  • Notification channels in SAP Fiori launchpad and emails

Situation Handling is designed to keep users well informed and support them in their daily work.

Situation Handling emails

One way of informing users about critical business situations is by email. Emails are triggered together with notifications and sent automatically when situations occur, provided the notification option is activated.

Activate emails together with notifications

Let’s look at the standard notification settings of a situation type and their impact on emails:

  • Notify all after creation / Processor only after update: An email is sent to all recipients when a situation has occurred and to the processor when a situation was updated
  • No notification after closing: No email is sent to anybody
  • Notify all after reopening: An email is sent to all recipients

You can also define custom notification settings and their related impact. Whenever a notification is sent, an email is sent as well.

Email templates are predefined

Situation emails use predefined templates that are automatically assigned to the situation type. This means no additional configuration is required.

The predefined email template consists of the email subject and the email body. Most parts of the email body are fixed and cannot be changed. This contains the greeting, the links to SAP Fiori launchpad and to detailed situation information as well as the source and the sign-off. The customizable email text is the one that describes the situation.

Adapt email subject and email text

You have two options to adapt the email template.

  • Reuse the notification title as email subject and the notification text in the email body.
  • Add a specific plain text to the email body and go with an SAP-defined email subject.

The situation type automatically inherits the setting of the situation template. You can change the settings in the situation type at any time. Afterwards, the new emails are generated according to the updated configuration.

If you change a situation template later, it has no effect on existing situation types.

Reuse notification title and text in email subject and body

You can reuse the notification title as the email subject, including variables such as plant, supplier, or contract ID. The reuse option also inserts the notification text into the email body, where variables can be used as well. The email preview shows how the email will look like.

Additionally, you can select if notifications are aggregated or not.

  • No Aggregation: Send a notification and email for every situation instance.
  • Aggregate by Field: Group notifications as well as emails by a selected field, for instance, supplier.
  • Aggregate All: Group all notifications as well as all emails.

Let’s check how the different settings affect the emails.

Non-aggregated notifications and emails

If you choose non-aggregated notifications, a separate notification and an email are sent for each situation. Initially, notification title and text are already copied from the situation title and text. Title and text can be also reused as the email subject and in the email body. This provides an efficient configuration experience.

The email template contains links to both, the SAP Fiori launchpad and the specific situation.

Reuse option: non-aggregated email

Aggregated notifications and emails

Reuse works the same way for aggregated notifications. Whether you aggregate all notifications or aggregate them by field, the title and text are copied to the email template’s subject and body.

For a meaningful representation, you can use only character-based variables such as strings. In addition, the count variable indicates how many situations are aggregated. If you select Aggregate by Field, you can use the aggregated by variable to show the field used for aggregation.

Because of the aggregation, the email template no longer includes a link to a specific situation; instead, it shows the generic link to SAP Fiori launchpad.

Reuse option: aggregated email by field

Aggregation by field allows more specific end user texts by including this field as variable.

If you aggregate all notifications and emails, the texts will be more generic.

Reuse option: aggregated email for all situations

Note: Be aware of the variables you are using in the email. They may contain personal data. Review the variables to prevent unintended exposure in emails. Also make sure that the data is managed in accordance with the legal requirements and business needs.

Specific email text with SAP-defined email subject

Alternatively, you can add plain texts without variables in the email body and use a default subject defined by SAP. This option makes sure that the email does not include business-sensitive data or personal data which may conflict with legal requirements or business needs.

You also get a preview of the email with this option. Let’s take a look at how this works in detail.

Non-aggregated notifications and emails

For non-aggregated notifications, an email is sent for each situation using the default subject and the embedded custom email text.

The email template includes links to both the SAP Fiori launchpad and the specific situation.

Custom option: non-aggregated email

Aggregated notifications and emails

Aggregated emails also have a default subject and a custom email text without variables.

Because the emails are aggregated, you no longer see the link to a specific situation in the email template but the generic link to SAP Fiori launchpad.

Custom option: aggregated email by field

Since the custom email texts do not contain variables, they remain generic for both aggregation options.

Custom option: aggregated email for all situations

Now you are all set and know how you can configure emails matching your business needs.

Further Information

You find more blog posts in the Best Practices section of Intelligent Situation Handling in the SAP Community. And you can visit SAP Help Portal for further guidance on Situation Handling.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer. In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool. The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

If you want to get an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 and surrounding solutions, check out the SAP Cloud ERP Early Release Series.

This series will showcase live sessions led by product experts across the SAP Cloud ERP portfolio on a broad range of innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Enjoy expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A. Read this blog post to learn more.

All sessions will be accessible via the SAP Learning Hub (access for SAP Customers and Partners. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login.). You can register for upcoming sessions via the registration page. All recordings of past events will be available here.

entry.title: What's new in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release
entry.content:
When you trace the evolution of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, a pattern emerges: every release compounds our momentum; continuous innovation on a solid core.

We’ve broadened business scope, deepened our ecosystem, simplified upgrades, and scaled across industries and regions. With the release of 2608, we’re taking the next confident step.

Introducing the Autonomous Enterprise at SAP Sapphire, Business AI moves from concept to companion (you are welcome to follow the link Autonomous Enterprise | SAP) — not a driver, but an intelligent co‑worker embedded in the ERP experience. It helps the business users work faster and smarter while they stay firmly in control. The best decisions still come from people; AI simply helps them get there sooner.

Marking our 35th major release in SaaS ERP, we’re pairing Business AI with continued substantive progress across the core of the business: Finance and Manufacturing, Engineering and Workplace Safety, and Professional Services. All that keeps our customers’ business moving.

 

As always, I wish I had more time to show you everything we've delivered in this release. So, take this video as an appetizer for our upcoming deep-dive blogs, where my colleagues and I will walk you through many more innovations across various lines of business.

 

 

SAP-managed scenarios

Before we start with Business AI, let me share some of the latest capabilities around SAP-managed scenarios. I believe this is something that delivers significant value to every customer who adopts more than just core finance processes, which is the case for most of our customers. We started introducing SAP-managed scenarios with 2602 and continue to expand the portfolio with additional scenarios, where SAP takes care of the setup and interoperability across the suite of solutions. Our Suite Product Management team has done a fantastic job in this area, delivering the first scenarios with big success.

Since the 2602 release, Early Adopter customers and partners have successfully adopted the first SAP-managed business scenarios:

  • SAP-managed Workforce Management enables seamless Hire to Retire processes based on consistent workforce and financial data across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP SuccessFactors.
  • SAP-managed Operational Procurement provides a connected Procure to Pay experience through SAP-managed integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Ariba Buying, and SAP Ariba Invoicing.

More SAP-managed innovation with 2608:

Enhancement of SAP-managed Hire to Retire

  • Expansion of SAP-managed Hire to Retire with SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding and SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking, extending end-to-end process coverage across the employee lifecycle.
  • Consolidated Time Recording for Professional Services, improving the user experience for time and activity recording.
  • Contingent Workers Management across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP SuccessFactors throughout the worker lifecycle.

Early Adopter Care for SAP-managed Lead to Cash

  • Early Adopter Care for SAP-managed integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, enabling a connected Sales Force Automation process with consistent data, improved sales visibility, and a seamless experience across front-office and back-office operations.

Strengthening SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as the core

  • SAP-managed integration with SAP Subscription Billing and SAP Market Rates Management. Embedded into the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition user experience, these capabilities enable seamless cross-solution processes from a single launchpad.
  • SAP-managed integration with SAP Taulia extends working capital management processes, helping customers reduce integration complexity and connect finance and treasury operations more seamlessly across solutions.


But now, let's move on to Business AI. Across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we're introducing specialized AI assistants and agents that automate routine tasks, provide intelligent recommendations, and support users in their everyday work. Here are a few highlights that stood out to me.

 

Business AI innovations

Before we dig deeper into the topic of individual Business AI innovations, let me make a clarifying statement.

Business AI and Business users are designed to be a duet, not a duel.

AI agents are becoming an increasingly important part of our product evolution. We are seeing an increasing number of specialized AI agents supporting business tasks, while users remain firmly in control of business decisions and approvals. Agents help automate repetitive activities, provide intelligent recommendations, and work together behind the scenes to make everyday business processes more efficient. This lifts the execution level while the business user is still in control. This very approach, combining intelligent automation with human expertise, you will see reflected throughout the 2608 release.

Here are a few agents that I am excited about in the 2608 release:

 

SAP User Experience

AI-assisted easy fill of SAP Fiori elements-based applications

We want everyone to feel at home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, so we’re making the user experience easier to learn and quicker to master. That’s why we created Fast with SAP Fiori: short, one‑minute videos that show you exactly how to get things done, from working with tables to setting default values. You can find the first 10 tips right in the Help menu of the SAP Fiori launchpad, with more on the way. They’re easy to watch, easy to share from the SAP Digital Library, and available in different languages. Don’t see the tip you need? Just send us an improvement request in the SAP CIP session “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, User Experience and Cross‑Topics.”

We’re also expanding our AI-assisted UX. I am happy to announce the general availability of the initial version of AI-assisted easy fill. Business users often spend unnecessary time transferring information from emails, meeting notes, or phone calls into ERP transactions—an effort that is both repetitive and prone to manual errors. Users can now simply describe the business update in natural language. SAP automatically populates the relevant fields, making data entry faster, more intuitive, and less prone to errors. It's a great example of how Business AI is simplifying everyday work and making SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition easier to use.

This feature is one of 17 base AI-assisted features available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608. If not yet done, make sure to sign the "Acceptance of SAP AI terms for Base AI-assisted features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" to use the generally available base features at no additional cost. 

If you want to be part of the beta testing of the latest AI innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, kindly sign-up here. 

 

Finance & Risk

Receivables & Payables Clearing Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Let’s continue with Finance. Anyone who worked in this function knows how much time disappears into matching and clearing open receivables and payables. With our new Receivables and Payables Clearing Agent, that work changes fundamentally. It analyses open items, proposes balanced clearing entries, explains every recommendation, and, once approved, supports automated posting. The outcome: a faster, more accurate clearing process that lifts finance productivity and helps shorten the financial close.

 

International Trade Classification Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Classifying products for international trade is a complex and highly regulated process that requires significant expertise and careful interpretation of customs regulations. The International Trade Classification Agent leverages Business AI to analyze product information, interpret trade regulations, and recommend appropriate customs classification codes together with the legal reasoning behind each recommendation. This helps trade specialists classify products more efficiently, create auditable decisions, reduce compliance risks, and strengthen supply chain resilience while significantly reducing the effort required for classification.

 

Statutory Report Analysis Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Preparing statutory reports often requires finance teams to manually compare reporting periods, investigate unexpected variances, and trace the root causes of unusual values across tax codes, business partners, and financial documents. The new Statutory Report Analysis Agent helps finance organizations review statutory reports faster, identify anomalies earlier, and improve compliance before submission. The AI agent automates this analysis by detecting outliers, identifying trends and irregularities, generating concise summaries of key findings, and recommending next steps for further investigation or validation. By reducing manual review effort and providing explainable, data-driven insights, the Statutory Report Analysis Agent helps organizations improve reporting accuracy, accelerate the declaration process, identify potential compliance risks earlier, and make more informed decisions before submitting statutory reports.

 

 

E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent (planned for 2608.2)

As e-invoicing mandates continue to expand worldwide, finance teams increasingly need to process and troubleshoot submission errors returned by tax authorities and e-invoicing networks under tight regulatory deadlines. The E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent helps organizations quickly identify, understand, and resolve errors during electronic invoice submission. The AI agent automatically classifies technical and business-related errors, distinguishes between infrastructure, connectivity, data quality, and financial posting issues, and provides guided root cause analysis together with contextual resolution recommendations. By standardizing error handling, reducing manual investigation, and delivering explainable recommendations with humans in the loop, the agent helps organizations accelerate issue resolution, improve compliance with government e-invoicing requirements, and reduce the risk of submission delays and regulatory penalties.

 

Manufacturing

Production Planning & Operations Agent

Manufacturing is another space where AI moves the needle. Our new Production Planning and Operations Agent equips supervisors to manage orders with greater confidence: it automatically checks component availability, flags scheduling conflicts, and even supports order release via natural language. By cutting routine manual work and surfacing the right insights at the right time, it lifts productivity, reduces downtime, and keeps operations running smoothly.

 

Further innovations across various LoBs

SAP Integrated Product Development

Handover from Formulation Recipe to S/4HANA Master Recipe

Bringing new products from development into manufacturing often requires manual handovers and multiple data transfers, increasing the risk of inconsistencies and delays. Handover to Master Recipe in SAP Integrated Product Development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition streamlines this transition by enabling production engineers to derive production-ready master recipes directly from product development data. The innovation automates the creation of master recipes, bills of materials (BOMs), and production versions while preserving the relationship between engineering and manufacturing data. By reducing manual effort and ensuring greater consistency across product development and production planning, organizations can accelerate manufacturing readiness, improve process reliability, and support a more seamless transition from product design to execution. For more information, consult our SAP Help Portal.

 

Sales

Supply block in a sales order to control subsequent activities

A single sales order can trigger a cascade of downstream activities, from available-to-promise (ATP) checks and material requirements planning (MRP) to logistics execution. Supply block in a sales order to control subsequent activities helps organizations ensure these processes only begin when order data is complete, validated, and ready for execution. This innovation introduces an automated, item-level supply block that can be configured based on business rules, approval workflows, credit checks, or custom validation logic. By temporarily pausing subsequent activities until all required validations have been completed, organizations can reduce operational risk, minimize manual intervention, improve visibility into order status, and help ensure more accurate, reliable, and efficient order fulfillment.

 

Environment, Health, and Safety

Maintain and view hazardous substance inventory

Another innovation focuses on workplace safety and regulatory compliance. The new Maintain and View Hazardous Substance Inventory applications provide a centralized view of hazardous substances, associated products, and safety information across the enterprise. Users can create and maintain comprehensive hazardous substance inventories, organize data by business area or location, manage inventory revisions, and quickly access key details such as hazardous ingredients and Globally Harmonized System (GHS) classifications. This helps organizations strengthen compliance, improve workplace safety, and manage safety-critical information more efficiently.

 

Professional Services

New and improved time-recording app for professional services projects with a smooth mobile experience

Let’s close with professional services. The redesigned Time Recording app brings a modern, consistent experience across desktop and mobile, with flexible entry, bulk edits, and personalized views that make logging project time fast and effortless. The payoff is clear: more accurate timesheets, less administrative work, and stronger billing and resource planning.

   

                                         

 

Service

Customer and product hierarchy pricing

Every customer expects repair pricing to be transparent, accurate, and aligned with the agreements they have negotiated. Customer Hierarchy and Product Hierarchy Related Pricing in In-House Repair Transactions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 enables organizations to apply pricing and discounts consistently across in-house repair processes based on customer and product hierarchies. By automatically determining the appropriate pricing conditions for different customer groups and product structures, the innovation eliminates manual pricing adjustments and helps ensure that negotiated agreements are applied correctly throughout the repair process. The result is more accurate invoicing, greater pricing consistency, an improved customer experience, and a smoother connection between service execution and billing.

 

Cross Topics – Data and Analytics

Central governance of product data and business partners including customer and supplier segments

High-quality master data is the foundation of every successful ERP system. The central governance of product data and business partners helps organizations establish trusted product, customer, supplier, and business partner data through workflow-driven governance, configurable validation rules, automated field value derivation, and flexible approval processes powered by SAP Build Process Automation. By improving data quality from the point of entry and reducing manual maintenance, organizations can streamline governance, strengthen compliance, and enable more reliable business processes and better decision-making across the enterprise.

 

 

Smart business runtime environment

The Smart business runtime environment introduces AI-generated summaries that help business users understand KPIs and reports faster. Business users often spend significant time analyzing dashboards, drilling down into reports, and interpreting large volumes of business data before they can identify trends or make informed decisions. With the Smart Business Runtime Environment, users can automatically generate concise, contextual summaries for SAP Smart Business KPIs and reports directly within SAP Fiori, highlighting key insights, anomalies, and important business trends. By transforming complex analytics into clear, shareable narratives, the innovation accelerates insight consumption, simplifies data interpretation, and enables faster, more informed decision-making across the organization.

 

Closing thoughts

Every release is an opportunity to make SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition smarter, simpler, and more valuable for our customers.

On one hand, we’re expanding the possibilities of Business AI with intelligent assistants and specialized agents. On the other, we’re doubling down on the core capabilities that help organizations run better every day.

Those above are my key innovations in the 2608 Release Highlights. I’m keen to hear what matters most to you.

I hope this introduction gave you some appetizers of what’s new — and spotlighted a few innovations you’ll want to explore further. For deeper insights, check out the “What's New in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” SAP Help page and join the conversation on SAP Community to share which innovations you’re most excited about.

In the coming weeks, we'll take a closer look at additional business- and industry-specific innovations in a series of follow-up posts here on SAP Community.  To stay up to date with future content, be sure to follow the PSCC_Enablement tag and keep an eye on upcoming posts.

Feel free to follow me at BeSchulze in the community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates. 

Until next time,

Bert

 

 

🎦Register for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series! 

Between July 13-17, we are hosting a series of 24 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. On our landing page you can register for the entire series or pick the sessions most relevant to you. 

🔎Find all our assets  

The release 2608 showcases more than 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: How SAP MDG lets you download and fix problem records directly from your process
entry.content:
Introduction

If you have ever run a mass change or consolidation process in SAP Master Data Governance, you know how valuable the validation step is. However, when the validation step stops due to errors, the erroneous records are stuck inside the system. Error messages are visible, but there was no way to extract, fix, and re-upload them.

And when a file upload fails due to invalid source IDs, all you get was a generic message like "Load contains invalid source IDs: 2". No details, no way to tell which records are actually affected. Either way, users end up rebuilding files manually.

This pain point was also one of the most strongly requested topics in SAP Customer Influence, with many customers highlighting the need for a smoother way to handle failed records. This was addressed, both following the same principle: giving a possibility to download a file of the problematic records, in exactly the format needed to fix and re-upload.

Feature 1: Export Process Data with Messages in the Validation Step

The Problem

In a consolidation or mass change process, the validation step checks the record correctness. When errors are found, the process stops and shows aggregated KPIs (e.g., "47 records with errors"). You can drill into the Details UI to see which records failed and why, but there was no way to export that subset for offline correction and re-upload.

What Was Delivered

The details UI of the validation step directly includes a new export action. When pressing the button it navigates to the export app and the filter for selected records is automatically set.

What it does:

  • Exports all records currently displayed in the validation step's overview tab
  • Includes the related messages (errors, warnings) as an additional sheet/section in the exported file
  • Uses the standard MDG export file format, the exported file can be edited offline and re-uploaded directly into a new import or mass processing run

After downloading the file errors and warnings are shown in the ! Messages ! tab of your excel sheet:

Availability

  • MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2022
  • SAP S/4HANA cloud public edition

Feature 2: Download Invalid Records During Upload

The Problem

When uploading a file for a mass process, the system performs a technical validation, e.g. it checks whether the IDs in the file exist in the system. If they do not, it raises a generic error:

"Load contains invalid source IDs: 3"

That is all the users got, just generic information, no more details. Which three records? Which IDs? Users had to manually inspect the file, compare it with the system, and figure it out themselves.

What Was Delivered

Whenever invalid records in a file are detected, it is possible now to export those records from the original file by an “Export”-button in File Import Log area.

The file is in the same format as the upload, so users can correct the records (fix or remove the invalid IDs) and re-upload without rebuilding the file from scratch.

Availability

  • MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 in classic mode
  • MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 in cloud-ready mode
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Why These Two Features Matter Together

Both features address the same need: when something goes wrong in an MDG process, users need a way to act quickly. With both features, you can download the problematic records as a file, fix them offline, and use that file as the starting point for a follow-up process.

Feature 1 helps when errors are found during the validation step. Feature 2 helps even earlier, when the file upload itself fails due to invalid IDs. In both cases, the result is the same: a downloadable file with exactly the records that need to be fixed. This means users no longer must rebuild files from scratch or investigate manually which records caused the error. This saves time, reduces the load on technical teams, and makes the whole process more reliable, especially when working with large data volumes where even a small error rate can mean hundreds of records to fix.

Have you ever used one of these features?

_________________

Further Links and Resources

  • SAP Master Data Governance community https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/master-data-governance
  • SAP Help Portal https://help.sap.com/docs/MASTER_DATA_GOVERNANCE - Various help documents for e.g. MDG on S/4HANA Cloud Private and Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP Influence Portal https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3497 - SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA: Submit new ideas or vote on existing requests to shape the MDG roadmap
  • SAP Learning https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-master-data-governance-on-sap-s-4hana - Learning content covering MDG strategy, domains and processes related to MDG central governance, consolidation and mass processing end-to-end
